---
title: "Numismática México — Year-End Holiday Sales Strategy"
description: "Tableau data story driving a marketing recommendation for a Mexican numismatics brand"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-title: "On this page"
    css: ../styles.css
    page-layout: full
engine: knitr
---

<a href="../index.html#projects" class="proj-back">← Back to Projects</a>

<div class="proj-hero">
  <div class="proj-tag">// Project 01 · Data Visualization & Marketing Analytics</div>
  <h1>Numismática México<br>Year-End Holiday Sales Strategy</h1>
  <p class="proj-lede">
    A Tableau-based data story analyzing four years of sales data for a Mexican
    numismatics retailer. The work identified the brand's "star product,"
    quantified the lift from paid advertising, mapped product preferences across
    states, and produced a focused marketing playbook for the Q4 holiday window.
  </p>
  <div class="proj-tags">
    <span>Tableau</span>
    <span>Data Visualization</span>
    <span>Marketing Analytics</span>
    <span>Storytelling</span>
    <span>Business Strategy</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="kpi-row">
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-num">$50K+</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Peak monthly profit (MXN) · Sept–Dec 2021</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-num">25%</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Share of Q4 profits from one star product</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-num">64%</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Star-product sales attributable to ads</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-num">$23K</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Cumulative ad-driven profit · Central Region</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="proj-section">

## Context

Numismática México is a retailer of commemorative Mexican coins and bills sold
to collectors across the country. Like many specialty retail businesses, its
revenue is heavily seasonal — the September-to-December window consistently
delivers the strongest profit. The question driving this analysis was simple:

<div class="callout-box">
<strong>How can the brand replicate (or beat) its 2021 holiday peak — and where
should the marketing budget go?</strong>
</div>

To answer it I built a multi-chapter Tableau story that walks a non-technical
stakeholder from the high-level seasonality picture down to product- and
state-level recommendations.

## The Data Story

The Tableau workbook is structured as a six-chapter narrative. Each chapter
isolates one decision-relevant insight, building toward the final
recommendations.

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-02.jpg" alt="Story agenda">
  <figcaption>Story structure — six chapters from seasonality to regional ad impact.</figcaption>
</figure>

## 1 · Seasonality is the foundation

The first chapter establishes that holiday sales are not an opinion but a
consistent, multi-year pattern. From September to December 2021 monthly profit
exceeded 50,000 MXN — the highest peak on record — confirming Q4 as the window
where the marketing spend should be concentrated.

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-05.jpg" alt="Year-end 2021 peak profits">
  <figcaption>Year-end 2021 recorded the highest sales profits on record.</figcaption>
</figure>

The natural counter-question — *"what happens after the peak?"* — is answered
in the next chapter. January 2022 dropped to ~15K MXN, but that drop is also
the moment paid advertising starts contributing for the first time.

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-06.jpg" alt="Sales decline in January 2022">
  <figcaption>Sharp post-holiday drop — but also the first month with ad-supported sales.</figcaption>
</figure>

## 2 · Advertising changes the curve

By March 2022 ad-supported sales overtook organic sales for the first time, and
by late July 2022 ad-attributed profit cleared 20K MXN. This is the most
important behavioral finding in the deck: paid advertising doesn't just add a
flat lift, it shifts the slope of the recovery after the holiday peak.

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-07.jpg" alt="Ad-supported sales surpass organic">
  <figcaption>Ad-supported profits surpass organic, then exceed 20K MXN by late July 2022.</figcaption>
</figure>

## 3 · There is a star product

Not every product contributed equally. The Commemorative 5, 10 & 40 Peso Bills
& Coins Binder generated **42,466 MXN** of profit between September and
December 2021 — roughly **25% of the entire Q4 total** (175,826 MXN).

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-08.jpg" alt="Star product accounts for 25% of Q4 profits">
  <figcaption>The Commemorative Binder alone delivered ~25% of Sept–Dec 2021 profit.</figcaption>
</figure>

When advertising entered the picture, this product responded most strongly:
**~64% of its post-launch sales are attributable to ads.**

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-09.jpg" alt="64% of star product sales are ad-driven">
  <figcaption>After ads launched, ~64% of star-product sales were advertising-driven.</figcaption>
</figure>

The same product held the #1 position across every period studied — an unusual
level of stability that makes it the natural anchor for any campaign.

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-11.jpg" alt="Top product consistency">
  <figcaption>The same product held the top-selling position across all measurement periods.</figcaption>
</figure>

::: {.callout-box}
**Caveat — Feb–Mar 2022 dip.** Ad attribution on the star product dipped
noticeably in this window. The recommendation includes a root-cause analysis
before scaling spend, since campaign fatigue, creative issues, or platform-side
attribution changes are all plausible explanations.
:::

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-10.jpg" alt="Advertising performance dip Feb-Mar 2022">
  <figcaption>Advertising impact dipped Feb–Mar 2022 — flagged for follow-up RCA.</figcaption>
</figure>

## 4 · Geography matters

The national star product is **not** the top performer in every state. A
state-by-state breakdown surfaces a second high-impact product that sits
*outside* the national Top 3 — an untapped opportunity for regional
campaigns.

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-12.jpg" alt="Nationwide product sales breakdown">
  <figcaption>Best-selling product varies by state — the national star isn't always the local star.</figcaption>
</figure>

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-13.jpg" alt="Key products identified">
  <figcaption>Two products with disproportionate impact — one of them outside the overall Top 3.</figcaption>
</figure>

Regional ad-driven profit is heavily concentrated. The Central Region alone has
generated approximately **23K MXN** in cumulative ad-attributed profit — a
clear signal of where the next campaign dollar earns the most.

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-15.jpg" alt="Regional advertising sales impact">
  <figcaption>Cumulative ad-driven profit by region — Central Region leads.</figcaption>
</figure>

## Strategic Recommendations

Three campaigns, sequenced by risk and confidence:

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-16.jpg" alt="Strategic recommendations summary">
  <figcaption>The three-campaign playbook: star product, Top 5 replication, regional focus.</figcaption>
</figure>

1. **Prioritize the star product in high-revenue states.** Highest-confidence
   move; the product has demonstrated price-insensitivity and a strong ad lift
   on a customer base that already recognizes the brand.
2. **Replicate the January star-product playbook across the broader catalog.**
   The proven-creative approach from the early-2022 campaign is the lowest-cost
   way to lift Top 5 products without rebuilding strategy from scratch.
3. **Run a Top‑5 product campaign in high-revenue states.** Combines (1) and
   (2) — uses the star product as the anchor and the Top 5 for cross-sell,
   targeting only the regions where the existing customer base already lifts
   ROAS.

<figure class="slide-figure">
  <img src="assets/numismatica/slide-17.jpg" alt="Key benefits of recommendations">
  <figcaption>Why each recommendation works — the underlying business levers.</figcaption>
</figure>

## Tools & approach

| Layer            | Tool                                                  |
|------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| Data prep        | Excel / CSV cleaning, Tableau Prep                    |
| Visualization    | Tableau Desktop (Tableau Story format)                |
| Narrative design | Six-chapter structure, decision-led question framing  |
| Delivery         | PowerPoint deck for stakeholder presentation          |

## Resources

- 📥 [Download the full presentation (.pptx)](files/DataVis_Project_EN.pptx)

</div>

<div class="site-footer">
  Last Updated: `r format(Sys.time(), "%B %d, %Y")` &nbsp;·&nbsp;
  Javier Bravo Bernal &nbsp;·&nbsp; Built with Quarto &amp; R
</div>
